danger_signals.meaning: Threat to player; collision causes damage or death

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards (spikes, bottomless pits, falling platforms)
danger_signals.meaning: Sudden lethal obstacles requiring precise platforming; high danger state

danger_signals.visual_form: Darker, more menacing visual design in later levels leading to final boss
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger; player approaching most dangerous zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Animal sprite disappears; Nemo reverts to base form
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of protective ability; increased vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Fed animal (frog, gorilla, mole, etc.) becomes rideable; distinctive sprite change
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary protection and enhanced abilities active

safety_signals.visual_form: Bright, colorful, cartoonish visual palette throughout most levels
safety_signals.meaning: Whimsical dream world; contextually safe despite platforming hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: Early/mid-game levels with lighter aesthetic and fewer hostile elements
safety_signals.meaning: Progression through safer dream areas before approaching Nightmare Land

safety_signals.visual_form: Keys scattered and collected throughout level
safety_signals.meaning: Clear objective completion; advancing safely toward exit

faction_indicators.color_identity: Pastel colors: pinks, blues, yellows, greens
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Morpheus (king figure); animal companions as allies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dreamy, whimsical level design; candy rewards; friendly animals; bright architectural elements

faction_indicators.color_identity: Darker palette; increased use of reds, blacks, purples
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nightmare King as final boss; menacing creature design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies become more grotesque and aggressive in appearance as player approaches Nightmare Land
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Later levels feature darker, more hostile visual design; architecture becomes more ominous; increased enemy density

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bright pastel colors, whimsical sprite design, cartoonish enemies, friendly animals available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Animals willing to be fed and ridden; enemies present but manageable; level design encourages exploration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Colors remain vibrant but architectural elements become more complex; hazards increase on-screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty curve increases; more instant-death traps introduced; enemy spawning intensifies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darker color palette dominates; visual design shifts toward menace; boss encounters indicated by distinct sprite/arena design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies become more aggressive; less reliance on animal companions due to puzzle-like level design; Nightmare King controls realm

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darkest, most visually distinct environment; Nightmare King sprite represents ultimate antagonist
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battle with pattern-based attacks (Manta Ray boss noted with region-specific pattern variation); rescue of Morpheus imminent upon victory

environmental_storytelling.example: Nemo must feed animals candy to gain their unique abilities (frog's jumping, gorilla's strength, mole's digging). This visually communicates symbiosis and the dream world's logic where befriending creatures grants power.
Transition from pastel Slumberland to darker Nightmare Land visually tells the story of progression toward danger and the Nightmare King's influence over the dream realm.
Keys scattered throughout levels and locked exits communicate a contained journey; the unknown lock count at exit creates tension and encourages thorough exploration.

Removal of Flip's cigar and smoking gorilla in US/EU versions demonstrates content adaptation; sprite mouth positioning in smoking gorilla reveals censorship without full redesign, telling a meta-narrative about regional expectations.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard jumping and movement; vulnerable to single hit; primary platforming mechanic
Enhanced abilities based on animal type; altered health pool; different movement patterns; temporary invulnerability window
Predictable movement patterns; infinite spawn behavior; collision triggers damage
Pattern-based attack sequences; region-specific variations indicate adaptive design; defeat signals progression milestone
Static or wandering; can be fed candy to activate
Rideable; execute specialized abilities; provide visual/behavioral feedback when damaged or defeated

notes: Little Nemo communicates world-state primarily through visual aesthetic shift and mechanical progression. The dream/nightmare dichotomy structures the entire game's manifestation layer: Slumberland uses bright, friendly visuals and rewarding animal mechanics to signal safety and progression, while Nightmare Land uses darker colors and increased hazard density to signal danger and escalating threat. The game's difficulty (noted as 'punishingly difficult' by retrospective critics) is visually reinforced through instant-death trap placement and enemy abundance rather than explicit danger telegraphing. Regional differences (cigar removal, title screen redesign) demonstrate how even visual communication can be culturally mediated. The key-hunting mechanic creates uncertainty about completion, which is narratively tied to the unknown lock count at each exit—a visual communication of puzzle state. Animal companions function as faction indicators of Slumberland's benevolent influence, while their absence or defeat signals vulnerability and Nightmare influence.
